prompts: DNA replication starts at the origin of replication, where the DNA helicase unwinds the double helix forming a replication fork. Single-strand binding proteins stabilize the unwound strands. Primase synthesizes RNA primers, which are extended by DNA polymerase. On the leading strand, DNA polymerase continuously synthesizes DNA in the 5' to 3' direction. On the lagging strand, replication is discontinuous, forming Okazaki fragments, which are later joined by DNA ligase. DNA polymerase also proofreads newly synthesized DNA to ensure accuracy.
